business.headline: Energy crisis: Is the worst now over? Sajjid Chinoy respondsPrices of commercial LPG were cut by Rs 183.50 per 19-kg cylinder on Wednesday – the first reduction this year following a series of hikes in the wake of the West Asia crisis that caused energy supply disruptions across the globe.

Red Bull, PepsiCo, Sting among 6 energy drink brands get FSSAI noticesThe FSSAI issued notices to six energy drink brands -- Red Bull Energy Drink, PepsiCo's Adrenaline Rush Energy Drink, Reliance Consumer Products' Campa Energy Drink Gold Boost, Sting Energy Drink, Hell Energy and Coca-Cola-backed Monster Energy -- over misbranding and misleading claims.

PPF, NSC, SCSS interest rates for July-September 2026 declared: Has anything changed?The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) will continue to offer 8.2%, the highest among the major small savings schemes.

Indian women are taking charge of their money. Here's how they are investingThe image of women as only careful savers is fast changing. Today, more Indian women are investing, exploring digital platforms and taking control of their financial future.

EPFO extends online service outage till July 2: What does it mean for members?EPAs part of the upgrade, members will not be able to submit fresh claims through the EPFO portal during the maintenance period. The organisation has also temporarily stopped the processing of claims while the migration work is underway.

Sensex rises over 440 points, Nifty closes above 24,000; IT stocks cap gainsFinancial, auto and FMCG stocks powered the rally, while weakness in IT shares and concerns over a weak monsoon kept investors cautious.

RBI's latest worry isn't inflation. It's artificial intelligenceArtificial intelligence is no longer just transforming banking—it is emerging as one of the biggest threats to the financial system. In its latest Financial Stability Report, the RBI has warned that AI-powered cyberattacks, dependence on common AI platforms and growing technology risks could pose fresh challenges for banks and financial institutions.

IndiGo's new budget-friendly 'Lite' fare for cabin baggage flyers takes off July 15According to the airline, the fare is designed for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage. By paying only for the services they use, customers can have greater control over their travel costs.

A rare NRI investment opportunity, lost in paperwork? Nithin Kamath thinks soThe Zerodha co-founder says lengthy onboarding processes may prevent NRIs from taking advantage of attractive FCNR deposit rates enabled by a recent RBI move.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee unions and staff bodies have asked the 8th Pay Commission to raise the minimum basic pay. The debate has widened into a question of inflation, family needs and a fair living wage.

7 big financial changes from July 1 every Indian must knowJuly has brought several important financial changes that could affect your everyday life. From ITR filing deadlines and passport fees to Aadhaar updates, banking rules and credit card benefits, here's what has changed from July 1.

Why India's energy scare may be ending sooner than expectedFuel prices have started easing in India as crude oil retreats, Nayara cuts pump rates and commercial LPG gets cheaper. The shift points to improving supplies after the West Asia conflict, though volatility remains.

Market open higher as auto stocks lead; monsoon worries keep gains in checkIndian benchmark indices opened higher as auto stocks led a recovery from Tuesday's losses. Gains stayed limited as monsoon concerns and stalled US-Iran talks kept investors cautious.

Gold prices fall, but buyers disappear as demand drops 70%Gold demand in India has fallen by over 70%, as higher import duty, subdued buying sentiment and a sharp correction in prices keep consumers away. With MCX gold down over Rs 50,000 from its record high, here's why demand has slumped and what experts expect next.

Nayara Energy cuts petrol price by Rs 5, diesel by Rs 3 as global oil rates coolNayara Energy has cut petrol prices by Rs 5 per litre and diesel by Rs 3 per litre across its nationwide retail network, becoming the first fuel retailer to reduce prices in over two years. The move follows a decline in global crude oil prices after easing tensions in West Asia.

Insurance mis-selling: IRDAI wants every policy linked to person who sold itIRDAI has proposed tagging every insurance policy to the individual who sold it instead of only the bank or intermediary. The move aims to improve accountability in insurance mis-selling cases and strengthen consumer protection.

Can you claim your EPF pension before 58? Here's what every employee must knowEarly retirement is becoming increasingly common, but what happens to your EPF pension if you stop working before 58? Can you start receiving it right away? Let us find out.

Price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder reduced by Rs 183. Check new ratesThe government has slashed the price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinders by Rs 183 in a big relief to customers after multiple hikes due to the West Asia crisis.

Trump earned over $1.4 billion from crypto ventures in 2025, filings showA law from 1978 mandates the President and the Vice President of the US to declare their income as well as their assets. Trump's latest filings detailed the fact that his companies received almost USD 800 million from World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture he and his sons co-founded.

Centre hikes special duty on petrol, cuts levy on diesel, aviation fuel exportsThe Centre has raised the windfall tax on petrol exports while cutting levies on diesel and ATF from July 1. The revision also widens export duty exemptions and leaves domestic fuel excise rates unchanged.

entertainment.headline: Ram Kapoor flaunts being 1st TV actor to perform lip kiss, says wife was proud Ram Kapoor has revealed how wife Gautami Kapoor reacted before his much-talked-about on-screen kiss with Sakshi Tanwar. His latest comments come amid the ongoing debate over his views on marriage and relationships.

Kala Hiran producer vows 8,000-screen release amid Salman Khan case Kala Hiran producer Amit Jani spoke about how they are planning to release the film amid the court case. The film has not yet been submitted to the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) for certification.

The Odyssey final trailer: Matt Damon leads epic Trojan Horse battle Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey released its final trailer on 1 July 2026. The footage underlines the scale of the IMAX retelling ahead of the film's 17 July release.

Ram Kapoor slammed for kissing co-contestant Varun 'Laila' Yadav in Lock Upp 2 A Lock Upp 2 episode showed actor Ram Kapoor grabbing Varun 'Laila' Yadav's face and kissing him during a conversation in the garden area. The moment drew sharp criticism online, with viewers raising questions of consent and conduct.

Raakh actor Ramandeep Yadav: I wanted Rajjo's eyes to make people uncomfortable Actor Ramandeep Yadav opened up about his year-long preparation for Raakh, studying criminal psychology, avoiding stereotypes, and overcoming typecasting.

Tragedy of Hera Pheri 3: Priyadarshan just confirmed what the fans feared most Director Priyadarshan has confirmed he is no longer associated with Hera Pheri 3 amid legal issues and personal conflicts. His exit has deepened fears that the long-delayed film may never reach screens, leaving a cherished franchise in limbo.

Gautami Kapoor reacts after Ram Kapoor's Lock Upp infidelity comment goes viral Actor Gautami Kapoor has reacted after her husband Ram Kapoor's controversial Lock Upp comments on infidelity and marriage went viral. Her latest Instagram posts have left fans talking.

Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married on July 3 or July 4? Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumoured July wedding has sent the internet into meltdown. Here's everything we know so far about the July 3 and July 4 speculation.

Exclusive: Ali Fazal on how Mirzapur The Movie is different from Mirzapur series In an exclusive interview, actor Ali Fazal said Mirzapur: The Movie is a completely fresh big-screen story. He rejected claims that the film is a spin-off of the series.

Fans dub Alia Bhatt 'Alpha mama' after she shares intense workout video. Watch Actor Alia Bhatt shared a high-intensity workout video as advance bookings for Alpha opened. The film also features actors Sharvari, Bobby Deol and Anil Kapoor in key roles.

Biggest box-office surprises of 2026 so far: Obsession, Karuppu to Haunted 3D A clutch of low-hype films from Hollywood, Hindi cinema and Tamil cinema has emerged as 2026's surprise box-office winners. Their runs showed that word of mouth, nostalgia and emotional pull could outpace star power and opening-day noise.

Ladies & Ladies: Yash, the toxic objectification of women and myth of female gaze Three years after promising a female gaze, Toxic's 'Ladies & Ladies' teaser introduces women only through Yash's desire - mistaking objectification for empowerment.

7 best stand-up comedy specials to watch on Netflix, Prime Video From Samay Raina and Vir Das to Trevor Noah and Taylor Tomlinson, these stand-up comedy specials promise plenty of laughs. Here's a list of seven shows across Netflix and Prime Video that are worth adding to your OTT watchlist.

Choreographer Bosco visits church after hospital discharge, shares health update Choreographer Bosco Martis has shared his first health update after being discharged from hospital following a week-long stay. His heartfelt message and visit to Mount Mary Basilica have drawn warm reactions from celebrities and fans.

Cheating got a makeover, ft. Ram Kapoor and others On Lock Upp, Ram Kapoor said cheating in a marriage is not a dealbreaker. Fine. Whatever works for him and others. But what is it with celebrities trying to normalise infidelity in these shows?

Kala Hiran row: Case deferred; makers say censor submission to happen after Monday The Delhi High Court deferred Salman Khan's plea against the makers of Kala Hiran to Monday after they assured the court there would be no release or CBFC submission till then. The case now centres on Khan's claim of a personality rights violation and the makers' recorded undertaking.
Exclusive: Rajkumar Hirani on Munna Bhai sequel, PK 2 with Ranbir Kapoor While promoting Pritam and Pedro, Rajkumar Hirani said Munna Bhai 3 and a possible PK sequel are both on his mind.

Actor Erica Fernandes returns to TV after 5 years with military-medical drama Erica Fernandes returns to television with 108 Base Hospital Uri, a medical-military drama set in an army hospital. The trailer highlights emergency trauma care, personal ties and the theme of Seva, Saahas and Samarpan.

Lenin trailer: Akhil Akkineni turns romance into a brutal village war Akhil Akkineni's Lenin trailer sets up a romance between Lenin and Bharathi that erupts into a violent village conflict. The film, delayed from its earlier date, is now due in theatres on July 10 with Nagarjuna promising a new Akhil.

general news.headline: Assam man provides 15 documents, but fails to prove he's an Indian. Here's whyA man in Assam submitted 15 documents, including 1951 NRC records containing his family's names, but was declared a foreigner. The Gauhati High Court has now upheld the decision by a Foreigners' Tribunal. This is why the man failed to prove his Indian citizenship.

Russia taps India for petrol to ease fuel shortages after Ukraine's drone strikesRussia has started importing petrol from India as fuel shortages spread across its 11 time zones, with rationing, long queues at filling stations and record petrol price increases reported in several regions.

Govt orders Meta to halt WhatsApp username rollout, seeks reply in 3 daysThe government's move came as WhatsApp prepares to introduce one of its biggest privacy changes in recent years by allowing users to connect through unique usernames instead of sharing their mobile phone numbers.

ENG 2 -1 DRC Live: Kane double seals comeback win for EnglandEngland (ENG) vs DR Congo (DRC) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ENG 1 - 1 DRC) Harry Kane has brought England roaring back into the contest after finally beating the inspired Dimitry Mpasi. With around 15 minutes plus stoppage time left, one moment of magic could decide who reaches the Round of 16.

10 to 6 in office, AC bus and Hindu funds: How CM Vijay silenced his criticsFrom the day he entered politics, C Joseph Vijay was dismissed by his critics as inexperienced and unfit to govern. Yet, Tamil Nadu voted him to power. Just as his films opened to blockbuster success, his first moves as the chief minister have won him praise and silenced his critics.

Toddlers stuffed into washing machine, sprayed with toilet jet at Bengaluru daycareThe alleged abuse came to light after videos purportedly showing the incidents went viral on social media. Police said they have issued notices to the five accused caregivers and begun an investigation into the allegations.

Red Bull, PepsiCo, Sting among 6 energy drink brands get FSSAI noticesThe FSSAI issued notices to six energy drink brands -- Red Bull Energy Drink, PepsiCo's Adrenaline Rush Energy Drink, Reliance Consumer Products' Campa Energy Drink Gold Boost, Sting Energy Drink, Hell Energy and Coca-Cola-backed Monster Energy -- over misbranding and misleading claims.

BAT-BMS app hits battery 3-wheelers with prank, but how does it switch off vehiclesA smartphone app meant for monitoring lithium batteries has gone viral after prank videos showed e-rickshaws being switched off mid-ride. Here is how BAT-BMS works, why only some vehicles are affected, and why the incident has raised fresh EV security concerns.

An open letter from 117 eminent India and Pak citizens to PM Modi, SharifMore than 100 eminent figures from India and Pakistan have urged PM Modi and Pakistani counterpart Shehbaz Sharif to restore dialogue and normal bilateral ties, including reinstating High Commissioners, resuming visa services and reopening airspace.

Vijay's TVK alleges bid to poach MLAs with Rs 35-crore offer, threats; 3 arrestedThe arrest of three people for 'horse-trading' bid targeting TVK MLAs triggered a political storm in Tamil Nadu, with TVK accusing the DMK of trying to topple the Vijay government.

Day 3 Live: Sabalenka, Sinner into Round 3, Mirra Andreeva in actionWimbledon 2026 live score and updates Day 3: Aryna Sabalenka and Jannik Sinner are through to the third round. Mirra Andreeva is up against Barbora Krejcikova. Later in the day, Novak Djokovic will be up against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

Cheating got a makeover, ft. Ram Kapoor and othersOn Lock Upp, Ram Kapoor said cheating in a marriage is not a dealbreaker. Fine. Whatever works for him and others. But what is it with celebrities trying to normalise infidelity in these shows?

Parliamentary panel may back removal of PM, CMs after 30 days in custody: SourcesSources said the Joint Parliamentary Committee is likely to retain the provision that would remove the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers and ministers after 30 consecutive days in custody in serious cases.

No Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debut in Durham as India ignore Gavaskar's suggestionThe wait for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut continues after the 15-year-old was left out of the first T20I against England despite growing calls for his inclusion. India stuck with their World Cup-winning core, ignoring Sunil Gavaskar's advice to hand the teenager an immediate debut.

Had I known about Siya's relation, I would have reconsidered marriage: FatherSia Goyal's father said her horoscope matched with Ketan Agarwal's before the engagement, with 27–28 gunas found to be compatible.

Why Delhi's new EV policy targets bikes first, not carsThe Delhi government has announced its electric vehicle (EV) policy that will stop registration of petrol two-wheelers from April 1, 2028. Why is the policy focusing on two-wheelers and three-wheelers first? Here's all you need to know.

Mumbai boy killed in tree fall cremated with the cricket bat he lovedVihaan Srivastava was so passionate about cricket. His family cremated him with a cricket bat after the boy was killed after a tree fell onto his school bus in Mumbai.

Crypto, global deals: Trump made White House comeback with a $2.2 billion jackpotDonald Trump's business earnings soared after he returned to office. His enterprises generated at least $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, up from a minimum of $622 million in 2024, according to The New York Times.

Mango diplomacy? Bangladesh sends a sweet message to Bengal CM amid border tensionsThe exchange of seasonal mangoes has long been an essential part of India-Bangladesh diplomacy. Former Bangladesh Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina utilised Hilsa and mangoes to strengthen ties with India.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office testRamayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

Mahua Moitra livestreams egg-and-brinjal attack targeting her at MLA officeMahua Moitra alleged that BJP goons threw eggs and brinjals at her Krishnanagar office. The incident added to familiar post-poll scenes of eggs being pelted at Trinamool leaders amid chants of 'chor, chor'.

Monsoon update: Red alert for Mumbai, Thane; heavy rains to lash MaharashtraMumbai and other nearby regions are expected to see heavy rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds, pointing to the strengthening monsoon season.

Amazon sued for Prime Video ads, charging extra to remove themAmazon is facing legal trouble in Australia after introducing ads to Prime Video and asking subscribers to pay extra for an ad-free experience.

sports.headline: Abhishek Sharma scripts history, becomes fastest batter to 100 T20I sixes Abhishek Sharma scripted history by becoming the fastest batter to 100 T20I sixes, reaching the milestone in just 785 balls before smashing a blistering 59 off 24 against England in the first T20I.

Jannik Sinner fights past Nuno Borges, Aryna Sabalenka survives McCartney Kessler scare Defending champion Jannik Sinner and world No.1 Aryna Sabalenka advanced to the Wimbledon third round after overcoming stern second-round tests on Wednesday. Sinner beat Nuno Borges in straight sets, while Sabalenka saved four set points before beating McCartney Kessler.

No Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debut in Durham as India ignore Gavaskar's suggestion The wait for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut continues after the 15-year-old was left out of the first T20I against England despite growing calls for his inclusion. India stuck with their World Cup-winning core, ignoring Sunil Gavaskar's advice to hand the teenager an immediate debut.

Naomi Osaka conquers Wimbledon nerves, keeps daughter's birthday plans intact 4-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka eased past Anastasia Gasanova to reach the Wimbledon third round before revealing her daughter's birthday had made her unusually nervous.

ENG 2 -1 DRC Live: Harry Kane on the double and England on top England (ENG) vs DR Congo (DRC) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ENG 1 - 1 DRC) Harry Kane has brought England roaring back into the contest after finally beating the inspired Dimitry Mpasi. With around 15 minutes plus stoppage time left, one moment of magic could decide who reaches the Round of 16.

Sooryavanshi should write exams, play gully cricket: Ex-cricketer fires a warning Former South Africa cricketer Daryll Cullinan has urged cricket to slow down on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi despite the clamour for his India debut. He argues the teenager needs space, exams and guidance before the IPL and online scrutiny harden brilliance into burden.

Injury blow for Serena Williams, doubles with sister Venus in doubt Wimbledon 2026: Serena Williams suffered a knee injury scare after her first-round loss to Maya Joint in singles. The setback has cast doubt over her scheduled doubles return with Venus Williams later this week.

Day 3 Live: Sabalenka, Sinner into Round 3, Mirra Andreeva in action Wimbledon 2026 live score and updates Day 3: Aryna Sabalenka and Jannik Sinner are through to the third round. Mirra Andreeva is up against Barbora Krejcikova. Later in the day, Novak Djokovic will be up against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

Uruguay did not want to learn: Bielsa bids goodbye with 100-min press conference Marcelo Bielsa signed off as Uruguay head coach with a brutally honest assessment of his tenure, saying his ideas never resonated with the squad as he reflected on the team's group-stage exit from the FIFA World Cup.

T20I captaincy was no surprise, it is not outlandish: Shreyas Iyer Speaking to the BCCI on the eve of India's T20I series in England, Shreyas Iyer said he was not surprised when the job came to him. Iyer replaced T20 World Cup-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav.

The Kylian Mbappe magic that made France's bereaved coach Deschamps smileKylian Mbappe's first instinct after scoring for France was not to celebrate with the crowd, but to run to Didier Deschamps. The emotional moment came just four days after the France coach returned to the World Cup camp following his mother's funeral.

The Vikings are coming: Haaland stands in the way of Brazil in FIFA World CupErling Haaland's winning goal sent Norway into the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The team will now face 5-time champions Brazil in their second knockout fixture.

How the relentless domestic circuit is shaping Indian athleticsIndian athletics is entering a new era where reputation offers no guarantees and domestic competition has become as ruthless as the global stage. At the Inter-State Championships in Bhubaneswar, established stars were pushed aside as emerging talent turned qualification battles into career-defining contests.

ICC rankings: Ishan Kishan replaces Abhishek Sharma as the new No. 1 T20I batterIshan Kishan has climbed to the top of the ICC Men's T20I batting rankings for the first time, replacing teammate Abhishek Sharma to become the world's No. 1 batter in the shortest format.

Watch: Jubilant fans take over streets of Mexico after historic World Cup winThe celebrations began inside the Azteca Stadium and quickly spilled onto the streets as Mexico ended a 40-year wait for a FIFA World Cup knockout victory. Fans across Mexico City erupted in celebration after El Tri booked their place in the Round of 16.

After embarrassing Ireland loss, did Shreyas Iyer snap at reporter?India are set for a quick turnover from the Ireland series as they take on England, starting July 1. Ahead of the opening game in Durham, fans have hit out at newly-appointed captain Shreyas Iyer for allegedly losing his cool against a journalist.

Jofra Archer tipped to test red-hot Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in England T20IsFormer England spinner Graeme Swann believes England will struggle to contain Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the upcoming T20I series, although he feels Jofra Archer could prove to be the teenager's toughest test.

Mexico secure first knockout win in 40 years, oust Ecuador to reach last 16Azteca was rocking long before kick-off and the celebrations continued well after the final whistle as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 to register their first FIFA World Cup knockout victory in 40 years and book a place in the last 16.

Another T20I. Same question. Will India hand Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut?England vs India, 1st T20I preview: As Shreyas Iyer's wounded India look to bounce back from a shock series defeat in Ireland, a powerhouse England side awaits in Durham with the clamour growing to hand 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut.

Serena Williams faces backlash after cancelling press conference at WimbledonSerena Williams came under fire on social media after cancelling her post-match press conference following a heartbreaking first-round exit at The Championships, Wimbledon on Tuesday.

Mbappe prioritises titles over Lionel Messi's all-time scoring recordKylian Mbappe brushed aside talk of Lionel Messi's World Cup scoring record after France's win over Sweden, insisting his only focus is leading Les Bleus to the FIFA World Cup final on July 19.

Henry explains why Olise, not Mbappe, is France's most important playerFrance great Thierry Henry believes Michael Olise has become Les Bleus' most important player after the Bayern Munich star starred in their 3-0 win over Sweden in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32. Kylian Mbappe scored twice to send France into the last 16, but Olise's two assists and all-round display stole the show.

Emotional Wawrinka fights back tears after final Wimbledon farewellWimbledon: Stan Wawrinka bid an emotional farewell after a battling four-set loss to Matteo Berrettini, fighting back tears as the crowd rose to applaud the three-time Grand Slam champion in his final appearance at the All England Club.

MEX 2-0 ECU Highlights: Clinical Mexico reach R16, Ecuador knocked outMexico (MEX) vs Ecuador (ECU) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico have won their first knockout game in 40 years. They beat Ecuador 2-0 in the R32 match and qualify for the next round without conceding a single goal. Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez's goals are enough for the co-hosts.

Koeman calls time on Netherlands managerial tenure after R32 World Cup exitRonald Koeman has stepped down as the Netherlands head coach after the Dutch were knocked out by Morocco in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, ending his second spell in charge after a disappointing campaign.

Mbappe makes World Cup knockouts his own as France crush Sweden in Round of 32FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe scored twice to extend his World Cup knockout scoring record as France beat Sweden 3-0 to reach the Round of 16, setting up a clash with Paraguay while moving within one goal of Lionel Messi's all-time tally.

Serena Williams loses on Wimbledon return, but daughter sees a champion's gritSerena Williams' long-awaited Grand Slam return ended in a three-set loss to Maya Joint, but the 23-time major champion reminded Centre Court of her trademark resilience before turning her focus to doubles.

'What's the point of VAR?' Drogba fumes after Ivory Coast's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup 2026: Didier Drogba blasted the officials after Ivory Coast were denied a penalty in their 2-1 World Cup defeat to Norway. The VAR call overshadowed Erling Haaland's late winner and Ivory Coast's exit.

FRA 3-0 SWE Highlights: Mbappe brace powers France through to Round of 16France (FRA) vs Sweden (SWE) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (FRA 3-0 SWE) After a barrage of chances by France to open the scoring, Kylian Mbappe finally opens the scoring at the stroke of half-time. Barcola doubled the lead in the second half. Mbappe makes it a brace as France completes a rout of Sweden in New York.

Haaland has last laugh as Norway spoil Diallo's dazzling display for Ivory CoastFIFA World Cup 2026: Amad Diallo's sensational cameo looked set to inspire Ivory Coast to a famous comeback before Erling Haaland struck late to send Norway into the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Norway will now face five-time champions Brazil in a blockbuster knockout clash.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
